entities.name: Charoen Pokphand Foods Public Company Limited

financial.note: Provides significant credit facilities for international M&A; rising interest rates increase the servicing cost of its high debt-to-EBITDA load (~8x-10x).
BBL is a core financier for CP Group's large-scale agro-industrial infrastructure; sensitive to policy rate hikes.

supply_chain.note: Subsidiary handling procurement of corn and soy; price volatility in feed ingredients directly impacts gross margins (feed accounts for ~50-60% of farm costs).
Operates 7-Eleven; serves as a primary retail outlet for CPF's ready-to-eat (RTE) meals and processed food products.
Makro and Lotus's are major volume buyers of CPF's fresh meat (pork/chicken) for the HORECA and retail segments.

competitors.note: Direct rival in premium meat and feed; competition for domestic market share in Thailand's livestock sector.
Competitor in poultry and swine; price wars in domestic carcasses negatively affect CPF's farm revenue.
Competitor specifically in the chicken export and processing segment.

macro_commodity_factors.logic: Direct revenue driver; 10% change in swine prices correlates with ~5% change in net profit.
Major raw material inputs for feed; higher global grain prices compress margins if output prices cannot be passed on.
62% of revenue is international; a weaker THB (higher USD/THB) results in higher translated earnings and export competitiveness.
China is a critical market for associates; oversupply or low prices in China (e.g., RMB <14/kg) lead to losses for CPF's China operations.
